title: Compact Public Track-Prior Table Per Race-Year
summary: Pirelli and F1 publish track characteristics that directly affect stint length and stop timing: tyre stress, downforce demand, track evolution, compound hardness, and pit-lane time loss.
rationale: Pirelli and F1 publish track characteristics that directly affect stint length and stop timing: tyre stress, downforce demand, track evolution, compound hardness, and pit-lane time loss. A small hand-built prior table may recover important omitted race context better than more generic nonlinear features.
implementation_hint: Create a race-year lookup with a few scalar priors from official previews or stable track metadata: pit-lane loss bucket, tyre-stress bucket, expected one-stop/two-stop bias, and soft-to-hard degradation tendency. Interact these with Compound, TyreLife, RaceProgress, and estimated laps remaining.
expected_effect: May improve generalization across circuits by shifting service-window thresholds in a race-specific way without needing many new raw features.
risk: Manual metadata is brittle and can overfit if the table becomes too detailed or inconsistent across years.
